name: Pumpkin Cheesecake with Maple/Bourbon Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 cups fine gingersnap crumbs (about 1/2 lb. gingersnaps)
1/2 pound cream cheese, at room temperature
1 cup unsweetened pumpkin puree (fresh or canned, not pumpkin pie filling)
recipeInstructions:
1. Lightly butter a 10x2 inch round cake pan.
2. Combine gingersnap crumbs, nuts, and melted butter in a bowl and mix well. Press the mixture firmly into the prepared pan and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
3. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees.
4. Make the Filling:
a. In a bowl, beat cream cheese until smooth.
b. Add sugars and spices (cinnamon, ginger, cloves, nutmeg), beating until light in texture, about 3-4 minutes.
c. Add pumpkin puree and mix until just blended.
d. Stir in eggs one at a time, scraping the sides of the bowl after each addition.
e. Stir in heavy cream.
5. Pour the batter into the crust and place the cake pan in a roasting pan. Add hot water to the roasting pan halfway up the sides of the cake pan.
6. Bake in the center of the oven until the cheesecake is firm to touch and slightly puffed, about 50 minutes.
7. Let the cheesecake cool on a wire rack for about 45 minutes. Cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours.
8. When the cheesecake is completely chilled, lower it into a hot water bath for 2-3 minutes to loosen the crust. Invert a large, flat plate on top of the cheesecake; invert the pan, and remove. Place a second plate under the cheesecake and turn it right side up.
9. Cut with a hot knife and serve with maple/bourbon sauce.
Cheesecake will keep for 4-5 days in the refrigerator and 2 weeks in the freezer.
